expectations.text: Produces a structured impact assessment (identifies impact level and risk level)
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 contains Step 1.2 'Impact Level: L1 (UI/interaction only)' and Step 1.3 'Risk Level: Low (Pure additions)' with detailed rationale for each.

expectations.text: Correctly classifies as L1/Low (UI-only, pure addition)
expectations.evidence: Step 1.2 says 'Impact Level: L1' matching definition exactly. Step 1.3 says 'Risk Level: Low' with rationale about no API/schema changes.

expectations.text: Decides to auto-approve (not pause for review)
expectations.evidence: Step 1.4 Decision Matrix shows 'Auto-approve'. Step 1.6 Gate says 'Low risk -> Proceed to Phase 2 immediately. No user approval needed.'

expectations.text: Plans to create or update a feature doc with acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 Step 2.1 creates 'docs/features/audit-log.md' with 6 acceptance criteria (AC-1 through AC-6), each testable and specific.

expectations.text: Includes a test plan (at minimum E2E tests)
expectations.evidence: Phase 4 Step 4.2 defines 4 E2E tests in settings.spec.ts covering all 6 ACs. Step 4.1 explicitly states 'None needed' for API tests with rationale.

expectations.text: Plans to update test-map.json for new/changed files
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 Step 3.2 discusses test-map.json, notes the parent path already covers it, and suggests an optional explicit entry for precision.

expectations.text: Includes a final verification step (full test suite run)
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 Step 5.2 explicitly lists 'python3 -m pytest api-tests/ -v --tb=short' and 'npx playwright test --reporter=list' with expected outcomes.

suggestions.assertion: Plans to update test-map.json for new/changed files
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes even when the plan merely mentions test-map.json without committing to update it. A stronger assertion would check if the plan specifies the exact mapping entry.

No assertion checks whether the plan explores existing codebase patterns (e.g., DataTable component, PaginationParams utility). The without-skill version discovered these reusable components while the with-skill version did not.
No assertion checks whether the plan reads the actual source files before proposing changes. The quality of the plan depends heavily on whether the agent understood the current code structure.

eval_feedback.overall: All 7 assertions pass, but they primarily check procedural compliance (did you do step X?) rather than plan quality (did you understand the codebase well enough to make good decisions?). Consider adding assertions for code exploration depth and alternative analysis.
